actual_terms_body_text:
Welcome to our application. By downloading, installing, or using this app, you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. Please read them carefully.

1. Use of the App
You agree to use this application only for lawful purposes and in a way that does not infringe the rights of, restrict, or inhibit anyone else's use and enjoyment of the app. Unauthorized copying, modification, or distribution of the app's source code is strictly prohibited.

2. User Accounts
To access certain features of the app, you may be required to create an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account details, including your password, and for all activities that occur under your account.

3. Intellectual Property
All content, trademarks, UI designs, graphics, and source code used within this application are the intellectual property of the developer and are protected by applicable copyright and trademark laws.

4. Limitation of Liability
This application is provided on an 'AS IS' and 'AS AVAILABLE' basis. We do not guarantee that the app will be completely free of errors, bugs, or interruptions. In no event shall the developer be liable for any direct, indirect, or incidental damages arising out of your use or inability to use the application.

5. Termination
We reserve the right to terminate or suspend your access to the application immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason whatsoever, including if you breach these Terms.

6. Changes to These Terms
We reserve the right to update or modify these terms at any time. Any changes will become effective immediately upon posting the updated terms inside the app. Continued use of the app signifies your acceptance of the updated terms.

privacy_policy_full_text:
This Privacy Policy describes how Dalel ("we", "our", or "the App") collects, uses, and shares your information when you use our mobile application. By downloading, installing, or using this app, you agree to the collection and use of information in accordance with this policy.

1. Information We Collect: We only collect information that is strictly necessary to provide and improve the services of Dalel. This may include basic usage data, device information (such as OS version and device model), and account details (such as your email and password) if you choose to register an account.

2. How We Use Your Information: The data we collect is utilized to maintain, personalize, and enhance your user experience inside the app, optimize performance, resolve system bugs, and communicate important updates or technical support notices to you.

3. Intellectual Property and Security: Your data security is highly important to us. All integrated systems, UI designs, and structures are built to protect your account details. While we strive to use commercially acceptable means to protect your personal information, remember that no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure.

4. Third-Party Services: The App may utilize reliable third-party services (such as Google Play Services or analytical infrastructure) that may collect data used to identify your device or track general usage performance to ensure smooth operations.

5. Changes to This Privacy Policy: We reserve the right to update or modify our Privacy Policy at any time. Any changes will become effective immediately upon posting the updated policy inside the app. Continued use of the app signifies your acceptance of the revised policy.

6. Contact Us: If you have any questions or suggestions about this Privacy Policy, please feel free to reach out to us through your official support email.
ancientEgyptDes: Ancient Egypt was one of the world's most magnificent and enduring civilizations, flourishing along the fertile banks of the Nile River for over three millennia. Beginning around 3100 BC with the unification of Upper and Lower Egypt, this extraordinary empire relied on the predictable flooding of the Nile, which provided abundant water, rich soil, and vital transportation routes. Ruled by powerful Pharaohs who were revered both as absolute kings and living gods, the civilization left an indelible mark on human history. They erected monumental stone structures, including the breathtaking pyramids of Giza—one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World—which served as eternal tombs for their rulers. Deeply spiritual, the ancient Egyptians developed a complex religion centered on polytheism and a profound belief in the afterlife, leading to the master science of mummification to preserve bodies for eternity. Utilizing hieroglyphics as a sophisticated writing system, they recorded an advanced society that made pioneering breakthroughs in medicine, mathematics, engineering, and astronomy. Today, the enduring legacy of ancient Egypt, from its grand temples to its mysterious treasures, continues to captivate and inspire the global imagination.
islamicEraDes: The Islamic Era in Egypt began a transformative chapter in 641 AD with the Arab conquest led by the visionary commander Amr ibn al-As, marking the transition of the region into a cornerstone of the Islamic world. The foundation of a new capital, Fustat, near modern-day Cairo, shifted Egypt into a bustling epicenter of global trade, vibrant culture, and profound scholarship, where the Arabic language and Islamic traditions gradually wove into the fabric of daily life. Over the centuries, Egypt was governed by a succession of major Islamic dynasties—including the Umayyads, Abbasids, Fatimids, Ayyubids, and Mamluks—each leaving an undeniable mark on the nation's identity. Cairo, founded in 969 AD by the Fatimids, rapidly evolved into one of the most prominent, wealthy, and culturally rich cities in the medieval world, anchored by the establishment of the prestigious Al-Azhar Mosque and University. Under the strategic leadership of Salah al-Din, Egypt served as a formidable fortress defending the region against external threats. Later, the Mamluk Sultanate ushered in a golden age of architecture, building spectacular mosques, schools, and complex fortifications that still dominate Cairo's skyline today. Blending strategic geographic advantages with immense achievements in science, medicine, philosophy, art, and literature, this era created a monumental cultural legacy that still draws millions of history enthusiasts from all corners of the globe.
battleOfMegiddoDes: Fought around 1457 BC, the Battle of Megiddo stands as one of the earliest, most meticulously recorded military conflicts in human history. Pharaoh Thutmose III, often regarded as the military genius of ancient Egypt, led his imperial forces against a powerful rebellious coalition of Canaanite city-states gathered near the strategic city of Megiddo. Outmaneuvering his enemies through a daring and unexpected march through a narrow mountain pass, Thutmose III caught the coalition completely by surprise, achieving a decisive and crushing victory. This brilliant campaign not only firmly secured Egyptian hegemony over the vital trade routes of Canaan and the Levant for generations but also cemented Thutmose III's legendary reputation as an exceptional military commander and strategist.
hyksosInvasionDes: The Hyksos Invasion represents a critical turning point during Egypt's Second Intermediate Period, around 1650 BC, when a diverse group of foreign peoples from western Asia migrated into the Nile Delta and successfully seized political control over Lower Egypt. The Hyksos brought with them revolutionary military technologies that changed Egyptian warfare forever, including horse-drawn chariots, composite bows, and advanced bronze weaponry. Despite decades of foreign occupation and fragmented rule, the native rulers of Thebes launched a fierce war of liberation. Ultimately, Pharaoh Ahmose I successfully expelled the Hyksos invaders, completely reunited the country, and established the New Kingdom—ushering in an unprecedented golden age of imperial expansion, wealth, and monumental construction.
battleOfJaffaDes: The historic Battle of Jaffa took place in the summer of 1192 as a climactic clash during the Third Crusade, showcasing the intense rivalry and mutual respect between two legendary leaders. After Sultan Saladin's forces successfully besieged and captured the strategic coastal city of Jaffa, King Richard I of England (the Lionheart) launched a daring, surprise amphibious counterattack with a remarkably small force. Fighting with legendary bravery, Richard managed to rout the Ayyubid forces and secure the city. Although the battle ended without a total military annihilation for either side, it highlighted the tactical stalemate between the two armies, directly paving the way for the historic Treaty of Jaffa, which granted Christian pilgrims safe access to the holy sites of Jerusalem.
battleOfHattinDes: The fateful Battle of Hattin, fought on a scorching July 4, 1187, was a monumental turning point that reshaped the geopolitical landscape of the medieval Levant. In a masterclass of psychological and desert warfare, the Ayyubid Sultan Saladin skillfully lured the massive, heavily armored Crusader armies into an arid, exposed plain near the twin hills known as the Horns of Hattin. By completely surrounding the Christian forces and cutting off their vital access to the nearby Sea of Galilee, Saladin's forces left the dehydrated Crusaders exhausted and vulnerable. The resulting decisive Muslim victory shattered the military backbone of the Kingdom of Jerusalem, directly enabling Saladin to recapture the Holy City of Jerusalem after nearly a century of Crusader rule and triggering the launch of the Third Crusade.
lionheartDes: King Richard I of England, immortalized as Richard the Lionheart, remains one of the most iconic, romanticized, and formidable military commanders in European history, best known for his leading role in the Third Crusade. Renowned across continents for his immense physical courage, tactical brilliance, and chivalric code, he waged an intense, high-stakes military campaign against Saladin in the Holy Land. Though his dream of recapturing the city of Jerusalem remained unfulfilled due to political divisions and supply shortages, his striking victories along the coast earned him the deep respect of his allies and enemies alike. His campaign concluded with the negotiation of the historic Treaty of Jaffa, ensuring long-term protection for Christian pilgrims visiting the holy places.
napoleonDes: Napoleon Bonaparte, the brilliant and fiercely ambitious French military general and future emperor, launched a daring military campaign in Egypt in 1798 aimed at disrupting British trade routes to India. While the military and geopolitical aspects of the campaign ultimately faced heavy setbacks and were short-lived, its scientific and cultural impact changed the field of history forever. Napoleon brought a massive contingent of over 150 scientists, engineers, and scholars (savants) who meticulously documented Egypt's natural history, geography, and antiquities. The crowning achievement of this expedition was the discovery of the Rosetta Stone, which provided the vital multilingual key for later scholars to finally decipher ancient Egyptian hieroglyphics, unlocking thousands of years of forgotten history.
saladinDes: Salah ad-Din al-Ayyubi, widely known in the West as Saladin, was the illustrious founder of the Ayyubid Dynasty and stands universally as one of the most revered military and political leaders in Islamic history. Renowned for his exceptional statesmanship, he successfully united the fractured Muslim lands of Egypt, Syria, and Mesopotamia into a powerful, cohesive empire. His ultimate military triumph came at the Battle of Hattin in 1187, which broke the Crusader hold on the region and allowed him to peacefully recapture Jerusalem. Beyond his unquestionable tactical genius, Saladin earned immortality and the deep respect of his Christian adversaries for his profound justice, immense generosity, and exemplary chivalry during an era defined by brutal conflict.
qianlongDes: The Qianlong Emperor was one of the most powerful, influential, and longest-reigning monarchs of China's Qing Dynasty, ruling for over six decades during the 18th century. Under his highly organized governance, the Chinese empire reached its physical zenith through successful military campaigns that vastly expanded its borders into Central Asia. This era was marked by immense economic prosperity, a booming population, and a magnificent cultural renaissance. As a deeply passionate patron of the arts, literature, and historical scholarship, Qianlong commissioned the collection of China's finest literary works, making his brilliant reign one of the most sophisticated, wealthy, and stable eras in the grand tapestry of Chinese history.
odysseyDes: The Odyssey is a monumental ancient Greek epic poem traditionally attributed to the legendary blind poet Homer, standing as a foundational pillar of Western literature. Sequenced as a sequel to The Iliad, the epic charts the extraordinary, tumultuous ten-year journey of King Odysseus of Ithaca as he struggles to return home to his faithful wife, Penelope, and son, Telemachus, following the fall of Troy. Cursed by the sea god Poseidon, Odysseus must rely on his legendary wit, cunning, and resilience to survive encounters with terrifying monsters, seductive sirens, vengeful gods, and treacherous sorceresses. Beyond the thrilling adventures, the poem deeply explores timeless themes of identity, loyalty, perseverance, hospitality, family, and the profound human longing for home.
gilgameshDes: The Epic of Gilgamesh stands proudly as the world's oldest surviving literary masterpiece, originating in ancient Mesopotamia and written on clay tablets in cuneiform script. The epic follows the transformative journey of Gilgamesh, the powerful but tyrannical King of Uruk, who is tamed through a deep, brotherhood bond with Enkidu, a wild man created by the gods. Following a series of heroic adventures against mythical beasts, Enkidu's tragic death shatters Gilgamesh, driving him into a desperate, grief-stricken quest to the ends of the earth to discover the secret to eternal life. Touching upon the raw truths of friendship, leadership, the fear of mortality, and the ultimate search for meaning, this primordial narrative continues to echo powerfully across thousands of years.
republicDes: The Republic is a towering philosophical masterpiece written by Plato around 375 BC, structured as a series of intellectual dialogues led by his mentor, Socrates. The text serves as a profound examination of the true nature of justice, the moral character of the individual, and the structure of an ideal, harmonious city-state ruled by wise 'philosopher-kings'. It is within these pages that Plato introduces some of the most influential concepts in intellectual history, including the famous Allegory of the Cave, which challenges our understanding of reality, education, and ultimate truth. Shaping centuries of political theory, ethics, and metaphysics, this work remains an essential guide for analyzing the responsibilities of rulers and citizens alike.
taoTeChingDes: The Tao Te Ching is a profound and beautifully cryptic ancient Chinese text traditionally attributed to the legendary sage Laozi, serving as the foundational scripture of Taoism. Composed of 81 short, poetic chapters, the text introduces the concept of the 'Tao' (The Way)—the indefinable, universal force that flows through and connects all things in existence. It advocates for a life lived in perfect harmony with nature, emphasizing the virtues of simplicity, humility, patience, and non-action (Wu Wei), which means moving with the natural flow of life rather than fighting against it. Teaching that true strength lies in gentleness and real power is found in inner peace, this timeless book continues to guide millions seeking balance in a chaotic world.
oldRingDes: The Old Ring is an exquisitely designed, vintage-style accessory meticulously crafted to evoke the mystique and artistic mastery of ancient civilizations. Featuring intricate engravings, weathered finishes, and symbolic motifs, its timeless design mirrors the regal elegance of historical signet rings once worn by powerful pharaohs, medieval knights, and noble rulers. Serving as a wearable piece of history, this beautiful ring acts as a tangible connection to the past and a meaningful statement piece for collectors, history enthusiasts, and anyone who appreciates the deep cultural heritage and craftsmanship of bygone eras.
miniStatueDes: The Mini Statue is a finely detailed, high-quality collectible sculpture inspired by the world's most iconic historical figures, legendary leaders, and monumental ancient art. Crafted with precision to capture the aesthetic spirit of classical antiquities, this miniature masterpiece is designed to elevate the ambiance of homes, offices, libraries, or personal study spaces. Offering a compact yet striking visual presence, it allows history enthusiasts to keep a physical piece of global artistic heritage close at hand, celebrating the enduring achievements and timeless beauty of humanity's greatest civilizations.
postersDes: This exclusive collection of historical posters brings the past to life through striking, high-definition artwork, vibrant colors, and historically accurate details. Featuring legendary military leaders, defining turning points in history, ancient world maps, and iconic architectural wonders, these posters are designed to educate, inspire, and decorate simultaneously. Whether used to spark curiosity in a classroom, add a sophisticated touch to a home library, or enhance a museum exhibit, each poster transforms walls into captivating visual stories that capture the essence of different historical eras.
oldMapsDes: Old Maps offer a fascinating, beautifully illustrated window into how our ancestors perceived, explored, and documented the world around them over the centuries. These high-quality reproductions display the shifting borders of ancient empires, forgotten geographic names, legendary sea monsters, and the vital maritime and overland trade routes that connected early civilizations. Serving both as valuable educational resources for understanding historical geography and as elegant, vintage decorative artwork, these maps are a perfect addition for anyone captivated by the spirit of exploration, discovery, and cartographic art.
